content: Hi, I'm Frankie, a data scientist at BioGenesis Pharma. I'm getting married next year and I want to learn some new skills to impress my fiancé.
Congratulations on your engagement, Frankie! What kind of skills are you thinking of learning, and how do you think they'll impress your fiancé?
I've always been interested in leatherworking and mountain biking. I think it would be great to learn how to make a custom leather wallet for my fiancé.
That's a wonderful idea! Leatherworking can be a great way to create unique and personalized gifts. Have you considered taking any classes or workshops to learn the basics of leatherworking?
I've actually taken a few classes at the local craft studio, but I'd love to get some more practice in. I'm also thinking about planning a mountain biking trip to Moab, Utah, for our honeymoon.
Moab is a great destination for mountain biking! The trails are beautiful and challenging. Are you planning on renting bikes or bringing your own?
I'm thinking about bringing my own bike, a Trek Fuel EX. I've also been thinking about learning how to bake sourdough bread to impress my fiancé with my cooking skills.

Baking sourdough bread can be a great way to impress your fiancé! It's a unique and delicious skill to have. Have you considered taking any classes or getting guidance from a baking expert to help with the process?
I've actually been working with a baking expert who specializes in sourdough bread. I'm hoping to have a few recipes perfected by the time we get married.
That's great to hear! It's always exciting to work on a project with a clear deadline. Are you planning on having any board game nights or game-themed events at your wedding?
We're actually planning on having a board game-themed bachelor party. I've heard great things about the board game cafe in town.
That sounds like a lot of fun! Board game cafes are a great way to spend time with friends and family. Have you considered playing any specific games or having any tournaments?
I've actually been thinking about playing some of my favorite games, such as Catan and Carcassonne. I'd love to get some recommendations from you on other games to play.
Those are both great games! I'd recommend checking out some of the newer releases, such as Gloomhaven or Scythe. They're both highly rated and a lot of fun to play.
I'll have to check those out. Thanks for the recommendations! I'm also thinking about planning a hiking trip to Zion National Park for our anniversary.

Zion is a beautiful park! The hiking trails are stunning and the scenery is breathtaking. Have you considered packing any specific gear or equipment for the trip, such as hiking boots or a portable stove?
